questions.question: What is the topic for Week 8?
questions.explanation: The topic provided for Lesson Eight is FAITH AND SCIENCE.

questions.question: Which of the following was the topic for Week 7?
questions.explanation: Forgiveness was the topic for Week 7.

questions.question: Bearing with one another, and ________ one another, if anyone has a complaint against another...
questions.explanation: The memory verse (Colossians 3:13) says 'and forgiving one another'.

questions.question: Provide the book, chapter, and verse for this week's memory verse.
questions.explanation: The memory verse is Colossians 3:13.

questions.question: Type out the memory verse for Week 8 in full (NKJV).
questions.answer: Bearing with one another, and forgiving one another, if anyone has a complaint against another; even as Christ forgave you, so you also must do.
questions.explanation: Full text recall for Colossians 3:13.

questions.question: According to the memory verse, what standard should we use when forgiving someone who we have a complaint against?
options: A. We should forgive as many times as they apologize
B. We should forgive even as Christ forgave us
C. We should forgive only if they pay us back
D. We should forgive if the church elders ask us to
questions.explanation: The verse states: 'even as Christ forgave you, so you also must do.'

questions.question: According to the introduction, faith and science are often portrayed as opposing forces.
questions.explanation: The intro states: 'Faith and science are often portrayed as opposing forces...'

questions.question: What does the assumption that belief in faith negates the validity of science overlook, according to the introduction?
options: A. The history of the church
B. The possible harmony between faith and science
C. The fact that science is a religion
D. The errors in scientific research
questions.explanation: The intro says 'This perception overlooks the possible harmony between faith and science.'

questions.question: What is the heading of the first outline?
questions.explanation: Outline I is titled 'THE CONCEPTS'.

questions.question: How does Outline I define faith?
options: A. The discovery and orderly classification of phenomena
B. A systematic study of the natural world through observation
C. A personal and spiritual belief, often centred on trust in a higher power or divine being
D. An outdated method of explaining weather patterns
questions.explanation: The text states 'Faith is a personal and spiritual belief, often centred on trust in a higher power...'

questions.question: Which scriptures are cited to support the idea that faith involves accepting truths that may not be practically provable?
questions.explanation: The text points to '(Daniel 3:16-18, Hebrews 11:3; Acts 9:3-6)' for this point.

questions.question: According to the lesson, science is defined as the discovery and orderly classification and exposition of the phenomena called nature.
questions.explanation: Outline I states exactly this definition.

questions.question: Which biblical figure used keen observation of the natural world to explain botanical, zoological and aquatic phenomena?
questions.explanation: The text says 'Solomon explained botanical, zoological and even aquatic phenomena (1 Kings 4:33).'

questions.question: Which biblical figure followed precise measurement and construction instructions revealing an innovative engineering skill that saved lives?
questions.explanation: The lesson lists 'Noah also followed precise measurement and construction instructions...'

questions.question: Which book of the Bible is noted in the lesson for containing profound observations of nature, animals, the weather and the cosmos?
questions.explanation: The text says 'The Book of Job contains profound observations of nature, animals, the weather and the cosmos (Job 38-39).'

questions.question: ________ was trained in the wisdom and knowledge of the Babylonians which included astronomy.
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states 'Daniel was trained in the wisdom and knowledge of the Babylonians which included astronomy.'

questions.question: What is the heading of the second outline?
questions.explanation: Outline II is titled 'THE HARMONY'.

questions.question: Which of the following historical scientific minds is NOT listed in the lesson as being a person of faith?
questions.explanation: Albert Einstein is not mentioned. The lesson lists Francis S. Collins, Isaac Newton, Galileo Galilei, and Gregor Mendel.

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to describe those who have placed their discoveries and inventions above the living God and refused to acknowledge Him?
questions.explanation: The text cites '(Psalm 53:1-2)' for those who refused to acknowledge Him.

questions.question: According to Outline II, the belief that the universe is orderly and governed by consistent laws stems from a faith-based worldview.
questions.explanation: The lesson explicitly says 'The belief that the universe is orderly... stems from a faith-based worldview'.

questions.question: The scientific discovery of the earth as spherical deepens the divine insight of which prophet, according to the lesson?
questions.explanation: The text states 'discovery of the earth as spherical by science deepens the divine insight of the prophet Isaiah (Isaiah 40:22).'

questions.question: While science helps explain the 'how' of natural phenomena, what does faith address?
questions.explanation: The text states 'While science helps explain the “how” of natural phenomena, faith addresses the “why”'.

questions.question: What example does the lesson give to contrast the 'how' of science and the 'why' of faith?
options: A. How birds fly versus why God created the sky
B. How the ocean tides work versus why God parted the Red Sea
C. How trees grow by respiration versus why God created them to provide food for man
D. How stars shine versus why God put them in the heavens
questions.explanation: The text uses the example of trees growing by sunlight/water/carbon dioxide (science) vs God creating them to provide food (faith) (Genesis 2:9a).

questions.question: According to the lesson, our belief in science should be absolute, while our faith in God should be intellectual.
questions.explanation: The lesson says the exact opposite: 'our faith in God should be absolute... while our belief in science should be intellectual.'

questions.question: Science is prone to ________ because it depends on limited mortal men.
questions.explanation: The text says 'it is also prone to errors because it depends on limited mortal men.'

questions.question: Which verses are cited to show that we can trust God as a perfect Creator?
questions.explanation: The text says 'since we trust that He is a perfect Creator (Deuteronomy 32:4, Psalm 18:30)'.

questions.question: What is the final conclusion of the lesson regarding faith and science?
options: A. Christians must reject science to preserve their faith
B. Science and faith will always be opposing forces
C. When approached with an open mind, faith and science can complementarily offer deeper insights into mysteries of existence
D. Science is completely irrelevant to the Christian worldview
questions.explanation: The conclusion explicitly states: 'When approached with an open mind, faith and science can complementarily offer deeper insights...'

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'Then the king instructed Ashpenaz, the master of his eunuchs, to bring some of the children of Israel...'
questions.verse_text: Then the king instructed Ashpenaz, the master of his eunuchs, to bring some of the children of Israel...
questions.explanation: This is verse 3 from the main Bible Passage.

questions.question: According to the Bible Passage (Daniel 1:4), what did the king want the young men to be taught?
options: B. The language and literature of the Chaldeans
D. The skills of chariot driving
questions.explanation: Daniel 1:4 ends with 'and whom they might teach the language and literature of the Chaldeans.'

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the qualities the young men in Daniel 1:4 were required to possess?
questions.explanation: The text lists 'no blemish, but good-looking, gifted in all wisdom, possessing knowledge and quick to understand'. Musical skill is not mentioned.

questions.question: The lesson asserts that faith provides meaning, purpose, and moral guidance.
questions.explanation: Outline I says 'For many, faith provides meaning, purpose and moral guidance'.

questions.question: The misconception that faith and science are mutually exclusive can lead to unnecessary tension.
questions.explanation: The text says 'These views can lead to unnecessary tension, playing down how faith and science can coexist.'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to show that discoveries in astronomy, biology, and physics can inspire awe and wonder?
questions.explanation: The text cites '(Psalm 19:1-6)' regarding inspiring awe and wonder in creation.

questions.question: Which verses are cited to show that understanding the vastness of the universe and complexity of life strengthens belief in a purposeful Creator?
questions.explanation: The text links this directly to '(Proverbs 3:19-20)'.

questions.question: Faith addresses the '________' providing a framework for understanding the deeper aspects of living things.
questions.explanation: The text states 'faith addresses the “why”'.
